headline: Pearson Names Certiport 2026 Adobe Certified Professional U.S. National Champion
summary: Pearson (FTSE: PSON.L) announced the winners of the 13th annual Certiport 2026 Adobe Certified Professional U.S. National Championship. Hosted in Nashville, TN, this year's event drew 41 finalists from thousands of students (ages 13–22) across the U.S. who qualified by earning an Adobe Certified Professional certification in Adobe Photoshop, Illustrator, or InDesign.

headline: Figma Stock Is Battered, but Citi Sees a Massive Turnaround Ahead
summary: The design-software maker has been battered by fears of artificial intelligence disruption and a broad sector downturn.  Figma shares were up 7.8% at $19.38 on Wednesday, snapping an 11-day losing streak.  The design software market faces unique risks, Radke conceded, as generative AI reduces the time and skill required for prototyping and ideation, in turn blurring the boundaries “of who a ‘designer’ is.”

headline: Jim Cramer on Adobe: “I Don’t Want You in It”
summary: Adobe Inc. (NASDAQ:ADBE) was among Jim Cramer’s stock calls on Mad Money, as he highlighted worthy space players and reviewed several of this year’s IPOs. A caller asked if Cramer sees any sign that the stock could pick up. He replied: No, the CFO just quit to go to Marvell Tech. The CEO has resigned. […]

headline: Adobe (ADBE) Launches AI Tools To Help Brands Compete Across Search And Commerce
summary: Adobe, listed as NasdaqGS:ADBE, has introduced Adobe Brand Visibility and new GenStudio features focused on AI-driven marketing and commerce media. The launch brings tools such as prompt analysis, automated brand optimization, and cross channel AI campaign insights to enterprise customers. These offerings are aimed at helping brands manage their visibility across AI search, content surfaces, and commerce media networks. Adobe is expanding its AI toolkit at a time when many marketers are...

headline: This Software Stock Is Down 52% in 2026. How AI Could Actually Save It.
summary: The design-software maker has been battered by fears of artificial intelligence disruption and a broad sector downturn.  That TAM is expected to double by 2029 as Figma expands its products and use cases, and as AI helps the company draw in less skilled users who weren’t previously part of its target audience.

headline: Anil Chakravarthy, Adobe President: Fortt Knox Innovation Lab
summary: Adobe President Anil Chakravarthy told Jon Fortt the company is launching Adobe Brand Visibility this week, a new product built on its just-closed Semrush acquisition that gives brands insight into how they show up across agentic-AI queries — the LLM-era equivalent of SEO. Chakravarthy also previewed an upcoming Adobe–Anthropic partnership embedding Claude into Adobe Express and Creative Cloud workflows for direct brand-to-AI integration.
